name: Oura Ring API
what_it_does: Provides access to Oura Ring biometric data including sleep stage analysis, HRV, body temperature, readiness scores, and daily activity metrics for personal health applications.
use_cases: Personal health monitoring dashboards and reporting
Sleep quality analysis and coaching applications
HRV-based recovery and training readiness tools
Integration with other health platforms for unified view
not_for: Medical diagnosis or clinical decision support (not FDA-cleared for clinical use)
Users without an Oura Ring device
High-frequency polling — data is daily summaries, not streaming
best_when: Building personal health or wellness applications for Oura Ring users who want automated analysis, reporting, or integration of their biometric data
avoid_when: You need real-time sensor data, clinical-grade medical data, or support for non-Oura wearables

auth.notes: Personal Access Tokens (PATs) available for personal use — ideal for agents. OAuth 2.0 available for third-party applications. PAT auth is simple and does not expire, making it agent-friendly.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5,000 requests per day on personal access plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise tier available for commercial applications with higher limits
pricing.notes: API requires Oura Ring device and active Oura subscription. Personal Access Tokens are free to generate. Enterprise/commercial use requires separate agreement.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP status codes with JSON error details; rate limit headers included in responses
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GET requests; fully idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Data availability depends on ring sync — data may lag by hours after collection
Daily rate limit of 5,000 requests applies across all scopes
No official SDK — must implement HTTP client directly
v1 API deprecated; ensure using v2 endpoints
Some data types (cardiovascular age, stress) require Oura+ subscription tier
PATs don't expire but must be generated manually by the user

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; sensitive biometric data; PATs are revocable; OAuth 2.0 for third-party apps; HIPAA-compliant data handling
